definition: used as a greeting or to begin a conversation
the earth and all people and things on it
an intense feeling of deep affection
the indefinite continued progress of existence and events in the past, present, and future
the condition that distinguishes animals and plants from inorganic matter, including the capacity for growth, reproduction, functional activity, and continual change preceding death
freedom from disturbance; tranquility
a state of armed conflict between different nations or states or different groups within a nation or state
the power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants without hindrance or restraint
just behavior or treatment
facts, information, and skills acquired through experience or education; the theoretical or practical understanding of a subject

a feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen
complete trust or confidence in someone or something
the state of being happy
the condition or quality of being sad
the accomplishment of an aim or purpose
lack of success
the act or instance of making or becoming different
the application of scientific knowledge for practical purposes, especially in industry
the imparting or exchanging of information by speaking, writing, or using some other medium

the expression or application of human creative skill and imagination, typically in a visual form such as painting or sculpture, producing works to be appreciated primarily for their beauty or emotional power
vocal or instrumental sounds (or both) combined in such a way as to produce beauty of form, harmony, and expression of emotion
the intellectual and practical activity encompassing the systematic study of the structure and behavior of the physical and natural world through observation and experiment
the phenomena of the physical world collectively, including plants, animals, the landscape, and other features and products of the earth, as opposed to humans or human creations
complete trust or confidence in someone or something
the emotions or conduct of friends; the state of being friends
a group consisting of parents and children living together in a household
a series of thoughts, images, and sensations occurring in a person's mind during sleep
the ability to do something that frightens one; bravery
the quality of having experience, knowledge, and good judgment; the quality of being wise
the quality or state of being true
a combination of qualities, such as shape, color, or form, that pleases the aesthetic senses, especially the sight
